counters.description: what can i say but don't take extended fights, and you only win all-ins against him with some help. instead, poke him often with your skills while watching for his "stand aside" ability, and make sure not to take too many empowered axes from him.

this matchup isn't hard, but bait his dash out before channelling your ultimate. make sure to poke him often using make it rain for good damage, and avoid taking long trades with lucian unless you get the upper hand.

watch out for nilah's jubilant veil (w), which dodges basic attacks and reduces magic damage & apotheosis (r) that interrupts your bullet times channel and pulls you close. preventing her from last hitting minions denies the bonus gold and experience from her passive. a tough matchup for you if you make too many mistakes.

she is very mobile with wild rush (resets on takedown), alongside her blade whirl that can block double up, bullet time and your auto attacks. both of these abilities help with stacking her passive. fighting samira is hard because she has safe laning with her flair ability and gets lifesteal early on to survive our poke damage. make sure to have healing reduction available to deal with her effectively.

it's important to note that while miss fortune has higher base damage in the game's early stages, senna has greater scaling potential. when facing senna, it's crucial to avoid getting hit by her abilities, such as her w, "last embrace," and her q, "piercing darkness," which can slow you down. additionally, piercing darkness can heal senna, while her ultimate ability, "dawning shadow," can provide her with a shield. therefore, keeping an eye on her positioning and coordinating with your team before engaging her is advisable.

this champion out-scales you if she gets ahead. her all-in potential is very potent, so avoid being shoved too far. it's best to play safe and get your support to bait out her skills, thus giving you a window to pounce on her after she is on cooldown.

this matchup is straightforward, dodge his w ability to avoid being affected by the poison and take a hit from his contaminate ability. be sure to watch out if he goes invisible too as he may bait you into an extended trade that might not be favourable.
he outranges you massively in the lane and has a larger poke radius with his q, e and r. due to this range difference you could get poked out and set behind.

just keep track of her movements and feather placements to avoid getting rooted by her ultimate and have it lead to you potentially dying. xayah's ultimate can dodge our abilities (becoming untargetable), so try to bait her ultimate before you engage for the best results.

what can i say but don't take extended fights, and you only win all-ins against him with some help. instead, poke him often with your skills while watching for his "stand aside" ability, and make sure not to take too many empowered axes from him.

this matchup isn't hard, but bait his dash out before channelling your ultimate. make sure to poke him often using make it rain for good damage, and avoid taking long trades with lucian unless you get the upper hand.

watch out for nilah's jubilant veil (w), which dodges basic attacks and reduces magic damage & apotheosis (r) that interrupts your bullet times channel and pulls you close. preventing her from last hitting minions denies the bonus gold and experience from her passive. a tough matchup for you if you make too many mistakes.

she is very mobile with wild rush (resets on takedown), alongside her blade whirl that can block double up, bullet time and your auto attacks. both of these abilities help with stacking her passive. fighting samira is hard because she has safe laning with her flair ability and gets lifesteal early on to survive our poke damage. make sure to have healing reduction available to deal with her effectively.

it's important to note that while miss fortune has higher base damage in the game's early stages, senna has greater scaling potential. when facing senna, it's crucial to avoid getting hit by her abilities, such as her w, "last embrace," and her q, "piercing darkness," which can slow you down. additionally, piercing darkness can heal senna, while her ultimate ability, "dawning shadow," can provide her with a shield. therefore, keeping an eye on her positioning and coordinating with your team before engaging her is advisable.

this champion out-scales you if she gets ahead. her all-in potential is very potent, so avoid being shoved too far. it's best to play safe and get your support to bait out her skills, thus giving you a window to pounce on her after she is on cooldown.

this matchup is straightforward, dodge his w ability to avoid being affected by the poison and take a hit from his contaminate ability. be sure to watch out if he goes invisible too as he may bait you into an extended trade that might not be favourable.
he outranges you massively in the lane and has a larger poke radius with his q, e and r. due to this range difference you could get poked out and set behind.

just keep track of her movements and feather placements to avoid getting rooted by her ultimate and have it lead to you potentially dying. xayah's ultimate can dodge our abilities (becoming untargetable), so try to bait her ultimate before you engage for the best results.

synergies.description: alistar's unique perk is his passive triumphant roar, which will constantly heal you so long as he gains the stacks (7) needed to activate it. in addition, his crowd control makes your kit function nicely and synergizes with your e/r combo.

amumu has excellent tools to help you snowball effectively and give you a gold lead on your opponent. his bandage toss & curse of the sad mummy abilities are great for locking down targets and making them susceptible to your make it rain/bullet time combo or hitting them with a double-up shot.

bard is great for the healing totems and the ult that puts you in stasis from harm or accidentally hinders your ult by mistake. the biggest thing you need to deal with when partnered with a bard is the roaming he does. talk to him to establish a plan of attack so you can base safely or survive while he's gone looking for his meeps.

unlike nautilus, blitzcrank's q gives him a hook that pulls the enemy toward him and his carry/team, his e is a knock-up, & finally, his r gives him the ability to silence and damage an enemy who is looking to pounce on you. the r from blitz can be great when setting up your ultimate after he does his initial q-e combo.

a burst mage that offers very little utility for you in the lane. he does have a decent poke that he can apply on the enemy bot and support. if they land a q stun after an e or w, prepare the cannons.

braum q and his passive allow a quick burst of damage after the stun is applied. his w can give you more resistance to physical and magic damage, his e shields you from incoming damage & his r knocks the enemy up, and he has great synergy when you ult.

galio is a strong tank that rarely sees much support play. his w is a taunt that is very helpful when gathering opponents to ult or even a single target you want to isolate, while his e is good at knocking up a target you want to chase down.

she has decent shielding potential with her e, good enemy disruption with her q and healing with her ultimate, making your lane phase poke heavy while still being healthy. additionally, janna can shield turrets giving you time to base and get back to the lane while minimizing turret and self-damage.

karma is a strong support option for miss fortune. she can empower all her abilities with added effects: q gets an enhanced slow, her w grants her a self-heal on contact, and her e-shield empowers all her nearby allies instead of one ally. great when looking to peel you out and set up kills in the lane.

leona is fantastic as support for miss fortune. she has plenty of ways to stun the opponent for you (q stun, e root and r stun/slow). in addition, she can give miss fortune great peel and target access to flashy ults or clean kills. so always look to get leona if you need a tank in the lane.

lulu has plenty of ways to keep you safe during the lane phase and fights: she can slow down the enemies with her fairy (pix), cast a polymorph spell on the enemy, and grant movement/attack speed and shielding to allies. another strong buff she provides is knocking up enemies around you with her wild growth ultimate, increasing your health, and slowing nearby enemies (while active).

lux q and e are good sources of poke for you to work off. it will allow you to walk up the lane and farm with ease. if she hits (q), you gain a big window where both you and she can help ult for massive damage.

maokai's strengths lie in his q knockback, w root and r root. make sure to coordinate your attacks carefully since maokai's abilities are very predictable and telegraphed. when damaging an enemy with his ult or (w), that is when you should unleash your ultimate and dish out some good damage.

milio is an enchanter support that provides healing, shielding, and a skill shot that stuns or knocks the enemy back. finally, his ultimate includes removing all crowd control you might be affected by. milio is an excellent option for miss fortune should you need healing and shielding in a pinch and should be requested as a support when dealing with heavy crowd control teams.

morgana is your typical dot mage support with heavy crowd control access. she has a 3-second q that can lock down any target it hits, a w that applies a dot burn, a black shield that will prevent you from any magic damage sources or crowd control spell that hits you, and a close-range lockdown ultimate that stuns if she keeps them in range long enough.

nami's healing and damage are potent when compared to other enchanter supports. she has a great peel with (q), a good heal spell with (w) and an (e) that will apply her spell effects. don't hesitate to fight the enemy when nami puts her e on you. her r knock-up will give a great window of opportunity to do considerable damage with your ultimate and act as a peel for you in a pinch.
nautilus q gives him a hook that pulls him closer to the target, (w) creates a root, his (e) a slow. lastly, his (r) allows you to isolate a running enemy or target someone looking to pounce on you.

neeko can be a solid support option for miss fortune. while she only possesses two displacement abilities (tangle-barbs [e] and pop blossom [ultimate]), she can surprise her enemies using these abilities with inherent glamour [passive]. due to neeko's lack of durability, pay close attention to when she gets an enemy isolated, as that can be your window for a quick trade or a kill.
point click w, shield blocking (e), and a strong poke from his (q) pantheon can do it all for you. once he hits (w), be ready to follow up and start punishing the enemy.

another strong support pick for miss fortune. poppy can lockdown targets with heroic charge (allowing for double up or bullet time follow-up) while neutralizing champions with dashes and leaps using steadfast presence. her best form of peel for you lies in her ultimate keeper's verdict, creating distance between you and targets.

when you have pyke as your duo, you need to understand the golden rule: he will pull them back to you with q and stun with e and set up kills for you pre-6. after he gets to level (6), it's a bidding war for kills, but don't fret. if pyke gets a kill and you were part of the takedown, you'll gain gold, so don't get too mad (even if it does) that he got the kill since you both are getting rich due to his passive.

rakan is a jack-of-all-trades support. his kit can heal you if he hits an enemy with q, knock them up with his w, shield you twice with e and charm them with r. rakan has the tools to make great plays while dealing damage in duels and team fights. however, he is squishy, be mindful of his positioning at all times.

rell provides great peel for you since she can give you armor and magic resistance aura with her e, and when close to enemies, she can stun them. additionally, with rell, her r can pull in enemies, which allows your e + r combo to be clean and precise.

her passive is similar to the imperial mandate item that gives you a burst of damage when you pop the mark. renata can grab them with a hook projectile, provide shielding and provide a two-part w ability: part 1 - attack speed/movement speed, part 2 - prevent your death so long as you get a takedown during that time. now, this is where she shines with you because her ultimate will make your ultimate hard to miss since she clusters the enemies and forces them to attack each other while you are hitting them with waves of cannon balls. she can create flashy highlights and could also provide a penta kill.

strong poke support that can heal, lockdown the opponent & cloak you in a self-peeling untargetable mist, then finally, a shield damaging ultimate. as far as synergy, there is little for you, but she's by no means a bad option to keep you healthy and gives a good setup for your kit and ultimate.

seraphine is the same idea as sona but with a different form of q poke, while her w doesn't heal as much but provides shielding for you in a pinch. the best thing about this duo is that seraphine's r pairs well with your r.

shen provides excellent crowd control and durability for miss fortune by blocking auto-attacks with his spirit's refuge and taunting enemies with shadow dash. he also offers shielding based on your missing health with stand united. to capitalize on shen's impact in the lane, coordinate his shadow dash with a follow-up bullet time, which can significantly damage the targets he taunts.

sona is your classic healer with good poke and strong sustain for you and her. the ability to make you faster and healthier means she is invaluable to your survival in the lane. her r synergies nicely with yours, so time them accordingly for massive damage.

strong support who can heal you on the fly, though at the cost of her health when using w. she heals herself with q (if the enemy is damaged) and others using her (w) global r ult. be mindful of her health bar before you fight so you don't kill her while she tries to heal you.

swain has two helpful forms of crowd control - a w that slows for you and an e that holds them in place for you, which is pleasant to have when looking to make a pick or when needing to escape combat. overall strong peel and decent damage in a pinch while being relatively tanky throughout the game as he acquires soul fragments with his passive.

pairing with tahm kench can have substantial results. his tongue lash and abyssal dive can be excellent ways to peel for you or create a setup for double up and bullet time. his ultimate (devour) offers free shielding (dodging auto attacks, abilities or crowd control effects that could harm you) and provides follow-up damage on the enemy once tahm spits them out.
taric is your best friend when it comes to protection in the game, he can heal on the spot, provide more armor/shielding for you, stun them for you and make you invulnerable when in a pinch.

thresh is a scaling support champion who has excellent synergy with miss fortune. he offers a lantern to click on for quick setups or getaways; he can hook enemies (using death sentence) and displace foes in any direction using flay. if he traps enemies within his ultimate (the box), the slow applied from its outer walls is an excellent setup for bullet time, double up or any other combo. what more could you want?
he has tons of poke-based abilities that can disrupt the enemy into positioning in bad spots. in addition, you can farm relatively safely with his high burst poke and assist him nicely during fights.
when paired with xerath, do your best to manage your lane state effectively. his powerful abilities can be beneficial in landing ults or even securing takedowns during trades and skirmishes.

yuumi grants you on-hit magic damage upon hitting the enemy with her q, on-hit healing with her w and shielding with her e when attached to you. although compared to most other supports, she may not be the best option for miss fortune, she can ensure you are healthy enough to last in long fights or skirmishes.
zilean provides you with faster levelling, increased movement speed when engaging and looking to disengage on his e and a mobile guardian angel r to get you out of sticky situations.

zyra's grasping roots ability is excellent for holding them in place for your make-it-rain/bullet time combo or landing a double-up bounce. additionally, her plants provide good harassment and damage over time to the enemy. if you are satisfied with this pick, coordinate a game plan to make your kits work together.

alistar's unique perk is his passive triumphant roar, which will constantly heal you so long as he gains the stacks (7) needed to activate it. in addition, his crowd control makes your kit function nicely and synergizes with your e/r combo.

amumu has excellent tools to help you snowball effectively and give you a gold lead on your opponent. his bandage toss & curse of the sad mummy abilities are great for locking down targets and making them susceptible to your make it rain/bullet time combo or hitting them with a double-up shot.

when you have pyke as your duo, you need to understand the golden rule: he will pull them back to you with q and stun with e and set up kills for you pre-6. after he gets to level (6), it's a bidding war for kills, but don't fret. if pyke gets a kill and you were part of the takedown, you'll gain gold, so don't get too mad (even if it does) that he got the kill since you both are getting rich due to his passive.

rakan is a jack-of-all-trades support. his kit can heal you if he hits an enemy with q, knock them up with his w, shield you twice with e and charm them with r. rakan has the tools to make great plays while dealing damage in duels and team fights. however, he is squishy, be mindful of his positioning at all times.

rell provides great peel for you since she can give you armor and magic resistance aura with her e, and when close to enemies, she can stun them. additionally, with rell, her r can pull in enemies, which allows your e + r combo to be clean and precise.

her passive is similar to the imperial mandate item that gives you a burst of damage when you pop the mark. renata can grab them with a hook projectile, provide shielding and provide a two-part w ability: part 1 - attack speed/movement speed, part 2 - prevent your death so long as you get a takedown during that time. now, this is where she shines with you because her ultimate will make your ultimate hard to miss since she clusters the enemies and forces them to attack each other while you are hitting them with waves of cannon balls. she can create flashy highlights and could also provide a penta kill.

strong poke support that can heal, lockdown the opponent & cloak you in a self-peeling untargetable mist, then finally, a shield damaging ultimate. as far as synergy, there is little for you, but she's by no means a bad option to keep you healthy and gives a good setup for your kit and ultimate.

seraphine is the same idea as sona but with a different form of q poke, while her w doesn't heal as much but provides shielding for you in a pinch. the best thing about this duo is that seraphine's r pairs well with your r.

shen provides excellent crowd control and durability for miss fortune by blocking auto-attacks with his spirit's refuge and taunting enemies with shadow dash. he also offers shielding based on your missing health with stand united. to capitalize on shen's impact in the lane, coordinate his shadow dash with a follow-up bullet time, which can significantly damage the targets he taunts.

sona is your classic healer with good poke and strong sustain for you and her. the ability to make you faster and healthier means she is invaluable to your survival in the lane. her r synergies nicely with yours, so time them accordingly for massive damage.

strong support who can heal you on the fly, though at the cost of her health when using w. she heals herself with q (if the enemy is damaged) and others using her (w) global r ult. be mindful of her health bar before you fight so you don't kill her while she tries to heal you.

swain has two helpful forms of crowd control - a w that slows for you and an e that holds them in place for you, which is pleasant to have when looking to make a pick or when needing to escape combat. overall strong peel and decent damage in a pinch while being relatively tanky throughout the game as he acquires soul fragments with his passive.

pairing with tahm kench can have substantial results. his tongue lash and abyssal dive can be excellent ways to peel for you or create a setup for double up and bullet time. his ultimate (devour) offers free shielding (dodging auto attacks, abilities or crowd control effects that could harm you) and provides follow-up damage on the enemy once tahm spits them out.
taric is your best friend when it comes to protection in the game, he can heal on the spot, provide more armor/shielding for you, stun them for you and make you invulnerable when in a pinch.

thresh is a scaling support champion who has excellent synergy with miss fortune. he offers a lantern to click on for quick setups or getaways; he can hook enemies (using death sentence) and displace foes in any direction using flay. if he traps enemies within his ultimate (the box), the slow applied from its outer walls is an excellent setup for bullet time, double up or any other combo. what more could you want?
he has tons of poke-based abilities that can disrupt the enemy into positioning in bad spots. in addition, you can farm relatively safely with his high burst poke and assist him nicely during fights.
when paired with xerath, do your best to manage your lane state effectively. his powerful abilities can be beneficial in landing ults or even securing takedowns during trades and skirmishes.

yuumi grants you on-hit magic damage upon hitting the enemy with her q, on-hit healing with her w and shielding with her e when attached to you. although compared to most other supports, she may not be the best option for miss fortune, she can ensure you are healthy enough to last in long fights or skirmishes.
zilean provides you with faster levelling, increased movement speed when engaging and looking to disengage on his e and a mobile guardian angel r to get you out of sticky situations.

zyra's grasping roots ability is excellent for holding them in place for your make-it-rain/bullet time combo or landing a double-up bounce. additionally, her plants provide good harassment and damage over time to the enemy. if you are satisfied with this pick, coordinate a game plan to make your kits work together.

alistar's unique perk is his passive triumphant roar, which will constantly heal you so long as he gains the stacks (7) needed to activate it. in addition, his crowd control makes your kit function nicely and synergizes with your e/r combo.

amumu has excellent tools to help you snowball effectively and give you a gold lead on your opponent. his bandage toss & curse of the sad mummy abilities are great for locking down targets and making them susceptible to your make it rain/bullet time combo or hitting them with a double-up shot.

bard is great for the healing totems and the ult that puts you in stasis from harm or accidentally hinders your ult by mistake. the biggest thing you need to deal with when partnered with a bard is the roaming he does. talk to him to establish a plan of attack so you can base safely or survive while he's gone looking for his meeps.

unlike nautilus, blitzcrank's q gives him a hook that pulls the enemy toward him and his carry/team, his e is a knock-up, & finally, his r gives him the ability to silence and damage an enemy who is looking to pounce on you. the r from blitz can be great when setting up your ultimate after he does his initial q-e combo.

a burst mage that offers very little utility for you in the lane. he does have a decent poke that he can apply on the enemy bot and support. if they land a q stun after an e or w, prepare the cannons.

braum q and his passive allow a quick burst of damage after the stun is applied. his w can give you more resistance to physical and magic damage, his e shields you from incoming damage & his r knocks the enemy up, and he has great synergy when you ult.

galio is a strong tank that rarely sees much support play. his w is a taunt that is very helpful when gathering opponents to ult or even a single target you want to isolate, while his e is good at knocking up a target you want to chase down.

she has decent shielding potential with her e, good enemy disruption with her q and healing with her ultimate, making your lane phase poke heavy while still being healthy. additionally, janna can shield turrets giving you time to base and get back to the lane while minimizing turret and self-damage.

karma is a strong support option for miss fortune. she can empower all her abilities with added effects: q gets an enhanced slow, her w grants her a self-heal on contact, and her e-shield empowers all her nearby allies instead of one ally. great when looking to peel you out and set up kills in the lane.

leona is fantastic as support for miss fortune. she has plenty of ways to stun the opponent for you (q stun, e root and r stun/slow). in addition, she can give miss fortune great peel and target access to flashy ults or clean kills. so always look to get leona if you need a tank in the lane.

lulu has plenty of ways to keep you safe during the lane phase and fights: she can slow down the enemies with her fairy (pix), cast a polymorph spell on the enemy, and grant movement/attack speed and shielding to allies. another strong buff she provides is knocking up enemies around you with her wild growth ultimate, increasing your health, and slowing nearby enemies (while active).

lux q and e are good sources of poke for you to work off. it will allow you to walk up the lane and farm with ease. if she hits (q), you gain a big window where both you and she can help ult for massive damage.

maokai's strengths lie in his q knockback, w root and r root. make sure to coordinate your attacks carefully since maokai's abilities are very predictable and telegraphed. when damaging an enemy with his ult or (w), that is when you should unleash your ultimate and dish out some good damage.

milio is an enchanter support that provides healing, shielding, and a skill shot that stuns or knocks the enemy back. finally, his ultimate includes removing all crowd control you might be affected by. milio is an excellent option for miss fortune should you need healing and shielding in a pinch and should be requested as a support when dealing with heavy crowd control teams.

morgana is your typical dot mage support with heavy crowd control access. she has a 3-second q that can lock down any target it hits, a w that applies a dot burn, a black shield that will prevent you from any magic damage sources or crowd control spell that hits you, and a close-range lockdown ultimate that stuns if she keeps them in range long enough.

nami's healing and damage are potent when compared to other enchanter supports. she has a great peel with (q), a good heal spell with (w) and an (e) that will apply her spell effects. don't hesitate to fight the enemy when nami puts her e on you. her r knock-up will give a great window of opportunity to do considerable damage with your ultimate and act as a peel for you in a pinch.
nautilus q gives him a hook that pulls him closer to the target, (w) creates a root, his (e) a slow. lastly, his (r) allows you to isolate a running enemy or target someone looking to pounce on you.

neeko can be a solid support option for miss fortune. while she only possesses two displacement abilities (tangle-barbs [e] and pop blossom [ultimate]), she can surprise her enemies using these abilities with inherent glamour [passive]. due to neeko's lack of durability, pay close attention to when she gets an enemy isolated, as that can be your window for a quick trade or a kill.
point click w, shield blocking (e), and a strong poke from his (q) pantheon can do it all for you. once he hits (w), be ready to follow up and start punishing the enemy.

another strong support pick for miss fortune. poppy can lockdown targets with heroic charge (allowing for double up or bullet time follow-up) while neutralizing champions with dashes and leaps using steadfast presence. her best form of peel for you lies in her ultimate keeper's verdict, creating distance between you and targets.

when you have pyke as your duo, you need to understand the golden rule: he will pull them back to you with q and stun with e and set up kills for you pre-6. after he gets to level (6), it's a bidding war for kills, but don't fret. if pyke gets a kill and you were part of the takedown, you'll gain gold, so don't get too mad (even if it does) that he got the kill since you both are getting rich due to his passive.
